mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending expectations and triggers short-term equity price appreciation within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.04 over 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during recessionary periods, or 20-day returns are negative in > 50% of triggered expansion-phase instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in monthly inflation triggers expectations of premature monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market strength rather than statistical artifacts, driving sustained positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or joint Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly increases corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns during GDP expansions are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing demand, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year window.
